name: Polygon.io API
what_it_does: Real-time and historical market data API covering US stocks, options, forex, and crypto with REST and WebSocket interfaces and official SDKs in four languages.
use_cases: Agents making trading decisions using real-time stock and options quotes
Portfolio analysis agents fetching OHLCV history for multiple tickers
Market research pipelines pulling earnings, company fundamentals, and news
Algorithmic trading bots streaming live trade and quote data via WebSocket
Options chain analysis for derivatives pricing and volatility surface modeling
not_for: Order execution — Polygon is data-only, not a broker
Non-US market data at scale (coverage outside US markets is limited)
Teams needing real-time data at zero cost (real-time requires paid plan)
best_when: An agent needs comprehensive, reliable US market data — stocks, options, or crypto — for analysis, signal generation, or real-time monitoring, and can afford a paid plan.
avoid_when: You only need a handful of price lookups per day and cannot afford $29/month — the free tier's 5-call/minute cap is functionally unusable for agents.

auth.notes: Single API key passed via Authorization Bearer header or apiKey query parameter. One key covers all endpoints. No per-endpoint scopes. Very agent-friendly — no token refresh needed.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 5 API calls/minute; end-of-day data only (15-min delayed); no WebSocket access
pricing.paid_tiers: Starter $29/month (unlimited calls, real-time data); Developer $79/month; Advanced $199/month (options, higher WebSocket concurrency)
pricing.notes: Free tier is too restricted for any meaningful agent workflow — real-time data and practical rate limits require paid. Starter at $29/month is reasonable for indie traders. Options data requires Advanced plan.

performance.rate_limits: Free: 5 requests/minute; Starter and above: unlimited REST calls; WebSocket concurrent connections vary by plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Consistent JSON error envelopes with status, request_id, and message fields. HTTP status codes are semantically correct (401, 403, 429, 500). Error bodies are machine-parseable.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All REST endpoints are read-only GET requests — fully idempotent. WebSocket subscriptions are stateful but reconnectable.
known_agent_gotchas: Free tier rate limit of 5/minute is effectively unusable for agents — budget for a paid plan before building
Real-time vs delayed data is plan-gated; agents must know which plan they are on to trust data freshness
Ticker symbols must be exact (AAPL, not Apple Inc.) — no fuzzy matching on most endpoints
Options symbols use OCC 21-character symbology (e.g., O:AAPL251219C00150000) — agents must construct or parse this format
WebSocket connections require reconnection logic; streams drop silently under network instability
Market hours affect data availability — after-hours quotes require checking the correct snapshot endpoint

security_score_components.security_notes: API key passed as query parameter is a security anti-pattern (appears in server logs, browser history). Bearer header method is preferable and available. No key rotation tooling exposed in dashboard.
